description: One-pass review of crate secretx-pkcs11 (secretx-pkcs11/). Filed by /review-rusty-crate. Crate scope: secretx-pkcs11/{Cargo.toml, README.md, src/lib.rs}.
notes:
[my-oss pass] Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — backend works for the SoftHSM2 + EC P-256 + RSA-PSS happy path covered by tests, but multiple realistic deployment scenarios (network HSM, transient disconnect, second backend instance, Ed25519, P-384, multi-process writes) produce silent or misleading failures. The crate is a v0 publication so breaking-change repairs are still cheap; landing the .1 (C_Initialize), .2 (Backend vs Unavailable), .7 (README pin), and .9/.10/.11 (algorithm gaps) findings before declaring the API stable would significantly cut the future-issue volume.
[my-oss pass] Correctness confidence: 70% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: .1 (C_Initialize collision — depends on cryptoki internal refcounting that I did not verify by reading cryptoki source), .14 (OS_LOCKING_OK assumption — depends on each PKCS#11 vendor's library behavior which I cannot enumerate). Uncertainty driven by: (a) no integration test run available in this review pass, only static read; (b) cryptoki crate behavior on Drop and on multi-Pkcs11-context not verified; (c) network-backed PKCS#11 proxies (gcp-kms-pkcs11, AWS CloudHSM client) have vendor-specific quirks not covered by the SoftHSM2-tested path.
[rusty pass] Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code is readable, error handling is consistent (even if stringly-typed), no unsafe blocks, no Box<dyn Error> in public API, derive macros not abused, no exported macro_rules!. The ergonomic gaps (clippy::ref_option, redundant repetitive patterns) are real but not embarrassing. Refactoring against findings .18, .19, .26, .28, .32, .34 plus the my-oss findings would land it at CLEAN.
[rusty pass] Rust idiom confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: .12 (PIN zeroization through cryptoki::types::AuthPin — depends on cryptoki impl I did not read), .13 (cryptoki::Pkcs11 Drop calling C_Finalize — same). Uncertainty driven by: cryptoki crate internals not inspected as part of this pass; verifying would require shallow-cloning parallaxsecond/rust-cryptoki and reading lib + types modules. Bikeshedding watch: findings .27, .30, .31, .33 are tagged opinion and should not be acted on without team buy-in.
title: Review: secretx-pkcs11 rusty pass
